title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Type-System Trace: Buffer Decoding Boundary
summary: Consumers parse Buffer content directly into trusted domain types, overload routing keys with identity data, lack stable event IDs and versions, and break when producers add or remove fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the buffer decoding boundary increment in versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Buffer Decoding Boundary · Theory Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5592 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
